task_completion.description: Select the primary network interface (eth0) for packet capture in Wireshark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wireshark's welcome screen or capture options showing 'eth0' selected or highlighted.

task_completion.description: Configure a strict capture filter for ICMP traffic before starting the capture.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The word 'icmp' typed into the Capture Filter input box (usually on the welcome screen or in Capture Options), which typically turns green when valid.

task_completion.description: Run the ping command in a terminal to generate ICMP traffic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing the command 'ping -c 10 8.8.8.8' being executed, along with the corresponding ping reply output.

task_completion.description: Capture live ICMP packets without any background HTTP/TCP noise.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Wireshark's packet list view showing captured ICMP packets (Echo (ping) request and reply). There should be no TCP, HTTP, or other protocol packets visible in the list.

task_completion.description: Save the captured packets to the specified file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box showing the directory '/home/ga/Documents/captures/' and the file name 'icmp_strict.pcapng', or a terminal/file manager verifying the file exists at this exact path.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a Capture Filter, not just a Display Filter.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure 'icmp' was entered in the Capture Filter box (before capture) rather than the Display Filter bar (above the packet list). A display filter only hides noise but still saves it to the file, which would fail the strict zero-noise requirement.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually interacted with the Wireshark GUI to configure the capture.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Wireshark application open and being actively configured, rather than just using a text editor or bypassing the GUI entirely.

integrity.description: Verify the ping traffic was genuinely generated in a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should show the actual execution and live output of the ping command, not a mocked text file or copy-pasted text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/captures/icmp_strict.pcapng'. The capture must be performed on the 'eth0' interface. A strict capture filter ('icmp') must be used to ensure exactly 0 non-ICMP packets are recorded. The final file must contain at least 10 ICMP packets, which are generated by the agent running 'ping -c 10 8.8.8.8' in a terminal.
